title: Classes, instances and attributes

sources.claim: A Python class defines behavior and shared class attributes, while each instance normally stores its own attributes; methods receive the instance explicitly as self and should preserve invariants.

sources.claim: A Python class defines behavior and shared class attributes, while each instance normally stores its own attributes; methods receive the instance explicitly as self and should preserve invariants.

canonicalExplanation.summary: A Python class defines behavior and shared class attributes, while each instance normally stores its own attributes; methods receive the instance explicitly as self and should preserve invariants.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A Counter initializes self.value per instance and exposes increment without storing the mutable count as one class attribute shared by every object. Your goal is to design and implement a class with clear invariants and no accidental shared mutable class state. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Scope and closures; Modules and imports; Function contracts and returns; pf data records. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Language-specific operational model
sections.body: A Python class defines behavior and shared class attributes, while each instance normally stores its own attributes; methods receive the instance explicitly as self and should preserve invariants. This model is applied through the declared Python: Beginner to Intermediate behavior rather than a language-neutral slogan.

sections.heading: Boundary and failure model
sections.body: Attribute access can invoke descriptors, class mutable values are shared, privacy is conventional, __slots__ changes storage rather than access control, and direct assignment can bypass ad-hoc validation. The learner must reject this documented misconception: An empty list declared in a class body becomes a separate list automatically for each new instance.

includes: Define instance state and methods while distinguishing class and instance attributes.
Design and implement a class with clear invariants and no accidental shared mutable class state.
excludes: Attribute access can invoke descriptors, class mutable values are shared, privacy is conventional, __slots__ changes storage rather than access control, and direct assignment can bypass ad-hoc validation.

outcomes: Design and implement a class with clear invariants and no accidental shared mutable class state.

examples.title: Classes, instances and attributes: direct language case
examples.situation: A Counter initializes self.value per instance and exposes increment without storing the mutable count as one class attribute shared by every object.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Observe the concrete Python: Beginner to Intermediate state or tool result: A Counter initializes self.value per instance and exposes increment without storing the mutable count as one class attribute shared by every object.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.

Step 3 — Verify the result with this correction: Class attributes are shared through the class; create per-instance mutable state on self during initialization and preserve invariants through focused methods.
examples.result: The example meets the atomic outcome because Class attributes are shared through the class; create per-instance mutable state on self during initialization and preserve invariants through focused methods.

examples.title: Classes, instances and attributes: boundary and transfer case
examples.situation: A class-level immutable unit label is shared intentionally, while each reading list is created inside __init__.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Name what changed from the direct case: A class-level immutable unit label is shared intentionally, while each reading list is created inside __init__.

Step 2 — Keep the language boundary explicit: Attribute access can invoke descriptors, class mutable values are shared, privacy is conventional, __slots__ changes storage rather than access control, and direct assignment can bypass ad-hoc validation.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting but incorrect rule: An empty list declared in a class body becomes a separate list automatically for each new instance.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Design and implement a class with clear invariants and no accidental shared mutable class state.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: An empty list declared in a class body becomes a separate list automatically for each new instance.
misconceptions.correction: Class attributes are shared through the class; create per-instance mutable state on self during initialization and preserve invariants through focused methods.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Predict both examples and identify the first observable contradiction in this belief: An empty list declared in a class body becomes a separate list automatically for each new instance.

analogy.example: a family of service records whose instance state, generated value methods, inheritance order, and protocols define observable behavior can introduce Classes, instances and attributes, after the canonical Python: Beginner to Intermediate rule is stated first.
usefulFor: classes, data classes, composition, inheritance, and special methods
limitations: descriptor lookup, MRO, hashing, NotImplemented, and shallow frozen state do not map cleanly to physical records

recap.summary: You should now be able to design and implement a class with clear invariants and no accidental shared mutable class state. Keep this boundary in view: Attribute access can invoke descriptors, class mutable values are shared, privacy is conventional, __slots__ changes storage rather than access control, and direct assignment can bypass ad-hoc validation.
